caveats: Low/base/high are near-term daily flow-equivalent judgment ranges, not observed cargo accounting. Public data support crude import and run cuts but do not separate government SPR, commercial inventory, bonded barrels, sanctioned barrels, or replacement-origin volumes.

Replacement estimate is anchored to the reported 15 percentage-point non-Hormuz sourcing shift, scaled conservatively to the EIA/Vortexa destination exposure row. India crude demand destruction evidence is weak compared with gas/LPG rationing evidence.

Replacement range maps METI's 60% May and 70%+ June alternative-procurement statements to the 2024 route-exposure row. Inventory draw is a bridge estimate from public/private reserve-release policy, not a measured drawdown series.

Korea has clear exposure and reserve capacity evidence but less public evidence on actual replacement crude volumes or demand curtailment. Treat as a preliminary bridge row until customs, refinery-run, or stock-draw data are reconciled.

EU aggregate direct crude exposure is moderate and replacement optionality is stronger than in Northeast Asia; Mediterranean refinery/product stress can be higher than the aggregate. Product row overlaps with crude and should not be added mechanically.

The U.S. can physically replace direct Gulf barrels, but consumers and firms still face global crude/product price pass-through. Inventory draw mixes SPR exchange availability and commercial stock movement; not all announced reserves become net supply.
